title: A net-new pizza audience to pitch your client
footer: WATT FOR AGENCIES: PIZZA CATEGORY PLAY

card.eyebrow: For DTC agencies · Pizza category play
card.title: A net-new pizza audience to pitch your client
card.dek: High-intent buyers your client's current targeting misses, and where to weight dine-in versus delivery.

blocks.title: A net-new pizza audience to pitch your client
blocks.dek: High-intent pizza buyers your client's current targeting misses, plus a state-by-state media plan for where to weight dine-in versus delivery.

blocks.title: The audience
blocks.lead: Net-new prospecting reach you can take into the pitch: everyone in-market for pizza, crossed with the behaviors that actually move reach, beyond the core the client already buys.

blocks.title: Composition
blocks.note: Curbside pickup, very-high-spend families, casual dining and price sensitivity were dropped: each added under 5% new reach because they already sat inside the bigger signals.

blocks.title: Where to build what
blocks.lead: Delivery intent outweighs dine-in roughly **4 to 1** nationally, so the map reads *relative* skew. The index is a state's dine-in share ÷ its delivery share × 100: above 100 → dine-in over-indexes (favor brick-and-mortar); below 100 → delivery over-indexes (favor ghost kitchens).

blocks.title: Market playbook
blocks.lead: How the index translates into the geo weighting you'd brief into the client's media plan.

items.label: Weight dine-in creative
items.desc: Brief in-restaurant creative and local store ads: the sit-down experience over-indexes here.

items.label: Split the budget
items.desc: Run dine-in and delivery in parallel: neither channel dominates among pizza households.

items.label: Weight delivery creative
items.desc: Lead with delivery offers and ghost-kitchen messaging where demand is already digital.

blocks.title: What to bring to the pitch

items.lead: Lead with the gap, not the obvious buyer.
items.body: The win is the under-tapped deal-seeker segment. That's the net-new reach the client isn't already paying for. Open the pitch there.
The audience anchors on coupon and deal behavior: a promo-led prospecting concept is the activation that reactivates these households.

items.lead: Build the media plan by market.
items.body: Weight dine-in creative in the dine-in states and delivery offers in the ghost-kitchen states, a geo plan the client can sign off on line by line.

items.lead: Pilot one region to prove ROAS.
items.body: Stand up a tighter high-intent cut in one market before scaling the full 13.2M audience on Meta or Google, proof in hand for the retention conversation.

blocks.body: Maybe the core. But the deal-seeker segment here is net-new reach their current targeting isn't buying. That gap is the pitch, not a confirmation of what they already run.

blocks.text: METHOD: Audience and channel splits measured on the Watt Signal Graph (US adults, refreshed Jun 2026). The siting index compares the state distribution of two deterministic 200K samples, pizza × casual-dining intent vs pizza × food-delivery intent, conditioned on pizza interest. States without enough sample in both audiences are left uncolored. Index = (dine-in share ÷ delivery share) × 100.
